text: How much are these apples? They look cheap and fresh.
That bag is expensive, but this one is cheap.
Excuse me, how much are these apples?
These apples are only three reais. They are very cheap today.
Great! And how much is that box of rice?
That one is expensive. But this brand here is cheaper.
Perfect. I will take the cheap one. Here is my money.
Thank you. Here is your receipt and your change. Have a nice day!
This one is cheap, but that one is expensive.
I will take this one, please.
Can I have my receipt and change, please?

Good afternoon, shoppers, and welcome to Saint Louis Supermarket. We have great prices for you today! In the fruit aisle, fresh apples are very cheap this week: only three reais for a bag. They are not expensive at all. In aisle four, you can find a special offer on healthy snacks. Take your cart and put a few items inside. At the checkout, our friendly cashier will help you. Remember to take your receipt, and don't forget your change. If you spend more than fifty reais, you get a free reusable bag. So, dear customer, fill your cart with fresh and healthy food. How much is it? Just ask, and we are happy to help. Thank you for shopping with us, and have a wonderful day!

Hi Carolina, it's Dad. I'm at the office, so can you go to the supermarket for me? I need a few things for dinner. First, please get some fresh vegetables and two apples. They are cheap this week, so it's a good price. Next, we need a small box of rice, but don't buy the expensive brand. Choose the cheaper one near the checkout. When you pay, the cashier will give you a receipt and your change. Please keep the receipt, because I want to see the prices. If you are not sure how much something is, just ask: How much is it? or How much are these? Take the cart, fill it carefully, and be a smart customer. Thank you so much, and see you at home!

Carolina goes shopping for food at the supermarket. First, she takes a cart and walks to the fruit aisle. Then, she asks the cashier, How much are these apples? After that, she chooses the cheap ones, not the expensive brand. Finally, she pays, takes her receipt and her change, and goes home with a bag of fresh food.
I want this bag here, please.
Those bananas over there are cheap.
The cashier gives me my change.
This one is cheap, but that one is expensive.
Can I have my receipt and change, please?
I will take this one, please.
